Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants a detailed explanation of a provided pseudo BPMN process The explanation needs to cover the purpose of each step the meaning of gateways path convergence looping differences between standard and custom paths and conditions for approvals rework

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Pseudo BPMN Input Start Event Task A Receive Customer Request XOR Gateway Check Request Type Path 1 Standard Task B1 Standard Validation AND Gateway Run Parallel Checks Task C1 Credit Check and Task C2 Inventory Check AND Join Task D Calculate Delivery Date Path 2 Custom Task B2 Custom Feasibility Analysis XOR Gateway Is Customization Feasible Yes Task E1 Prepare Custom Quotation No Task E2 Send Rejection Notice End Event Convergence Implicit Join after Standard Custom paths XOR Gateway Is Approval Needed Yes Task F Obtain Manager Approval XOR Gateway Is Approval Granted Yes Task G Generate Final Invoice No Task H Re evaluate Conditions Loop back to Task E1 for Custom Path or Task D for Standard Path No Task G Generate Final Invoice Task I Send Confirmation to Customer End Event

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Structure the Response A logical flow for the explanation would be High Level Overview Briefly summarize what the process is about Order Request Fulfillment Step by Step Breakdown Walk through the process chronologically Initiation and Routing The Standard Path The Custom Path Convergence and Approval Phase Finalization Gateway Explanations Explicitly define what XOR and AND mean in this context Standard vs Custom Differences Directly answer the prompts specific question Approvals and Rework Looping Conditions Directly answer the prompts specific question

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Critique Refinement Optional but adds value Point out any logical flaws in the pseudo BPMN for example the loop back from H to E1 D bypasses the approval gateway which might cause an infinite loop or skip necessary steps the implicit join before the approval gateway needs to handle the Custom No path correctly Self correction The prompt asks to explain the provided model but pointing out structural nuances shows deep understanding

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Step by Step Breakdown Start and Task A Triggered by a customer Task A captures the details First XOR Request Type Routes based on Standard vs Custom Standard Path B1 basic checks AND gateway splits into C1 Credit and C2 Inventory They happen simultaneously AND join waits for both Task D calculates delivery Custom Path B2 feasibility XOR checks if it is doable If no E2 rejects and ends If yes E1 creates a quote Convergence Both successful paths Standard after D Custom after E1 merge Approval Phase XOR checks if approval is needed likely based on price risk or discount If yes Task F Manager approval XOR checks result If yes Task G Invoice If no Task H Rework If no approval needed initially go straight to Task G Finalization Task I sends confirmation End

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Gateway Meanings XOR Exclusive One and only one path Used for decision making Standard vs Custom Feasible vs Not Approved vs Rejected AND Parallel All paths simultaneously Used for concurrent tasks Credit and Inventory Includes a split and a join

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Standard vs Custom Differences Standard Focuses on automated routine checks credit inventory and calculating a delivery date Assumes the product or service is predefined Custom Focuses on feasibility and quotation Requires human or technical analysis to see if it can even be done and pricing it out Has a hard exit rejection if not feasible

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Approvals and Rework Looping Trigger for Approval Likely based on business rules for example order value greater than threshold custom requests low credit score Trigger for Rework Manager rejects the request in Task F The Loop Task H Re evaluate sends the flow back to Task D Standard or Task E1 Custom Note I should mention that looping back to D E1 means it will eventually hit the Approval Gateway again so the re evaluated conditions must actually change the outcome otherwise it is an infinite loop

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Review and Refine Check against all prompt requirements Detailed explanation Yes Purpose of each step Yes Meaning of gateways Yes Convergence looping Yes Standard vs Custom differences Yes Approval rework conditions Yes Tone Professional analytical clear Formatting Use bolding bullet points and headers to make it readable

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Polish ensuring all BPMN terminology like Exclusive Gateway and Parallel Gateway is used correctly alongside the pseudo code terms Keep the explanation intuitive for a business analyst or process owner Ensure the distinction between the happy path and exception paths is clear Provide the Process Modeling Observations at the end to show expert level understanding

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self Correction during drafting Make sure to clearly state that the AND gateway requires BOTH C1 and C2 to finish before moving to D This is a common stumbling block in BPMN Done Include clear headings Done Ensure the loop back logic is explicitly mapped Done

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait the prompt specifically asks to clarify how tasks differ based on whether the customer request is standard or custom I will make a dedicated section for this Same for approvals Done All set Keep it structured Provide the final response
